meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 4 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: What is the equilibrium constant expression K for the reaction PCl5 ⇌ PCl3 + Cl2?
questions.explanation: K places products over reactants, each concentration raised to its coefficient: K = [PCl3][Cl2] / [PCl5].
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 0.5 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 5 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 1 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (5)(0.5)/1 = 2.5 m/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of electrons in a d subshell?
questions.explanation: A d subshell holds at most 10 electrons.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object starts at 5 m/s and accelerates uniformly at 2 m/s^2 for 5 s. What is its final velocity?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A 10 μF capacitor is charged to 100 V. How much charge does it store?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is 60 cm from a thin converging lens of focal length 20 cm. Where is the image formed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in C2H2 (ethyne)?
questions.explanation: The electron-domain geometry of C2H2 (ethyne) corresponds to sp hybridization of the central atom.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, removing a reactant from a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: removing a reactant from a system at equilibrium shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 0.5 m/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 2 m^2. What is the volume flow rate?
questions.explanation: Flow rate Q = A v = (2)(0.5) = 1 m^3/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the atomic number of carbon?
questions.explanation: The atomic number equals the number of protons; carbon has 6.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object with initial velocity 2 m/s accelerates at 3 m/s^2 for 6 s. How far does it travel?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two parallel plates 0.1 m apart have a potential difference of 12 V. What is the electric field between them?
questions.explanation: For parallel plates, E = V / d = 12 / 0.1 = 120 V/m.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a lens, the object distance is 10 cm and the image distance is 60 cm. What is the magnification?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral nitrogen (N) atom have?
questions.explanation: nitrogen (N) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 5.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass of 4 mol of HCl? (molar mass 36.5 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 4 x 36.5 = 146 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A solution has pH = 4. What is its hydrogen ion concentration [H+]?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which factor does NOT increase the rate of a typical reaction?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increasing the surface area of a solid reactant.

questions.explanation: Rate rises with concentration, temperature, catalysts, and surface area; lowering temperature slows it.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Heat spontaneously flows

choices.label: A
choices.text: from low to high pressure regions.

choices.label: B
choices.text: only when the objects are the same temperature.

choices.label: C
choices.text: from a colder object to a hotter object.

choices.label: D
choices.text: from a hotter object to a colder object.

questions.explanation: Without external work, heat flows from high to low temperature until equilibrium.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the ground-state electron configuration of a neutral carbon (Z=6) atom?
questions.explanation: Filling orbitals by the Aufbau principle gives carbon: 1s2 2s2 2p2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Starting at 10 m/s, an object accelerates at 5 m/s^2 over a distance of 5 m. What is its final speed?

questions.stem: How much energy is stored in a 2 μF capacitor charged to 5 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: After 3 half-lives, what fraction of a radioactive sample remains?
questions.explanation: Each half-life halves the amount: fraction = (1/2)^3 = 0.125.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in KF has an electronegativity difference of about 3.2. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 3.2 indicates a ionic bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 1 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 1 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 6.02 x 10^23.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H+] = 1 x 10^-4 M?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is needed to raise 300 g of water by 2 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 500 J of heat and does 300 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 500 - 300 = 200 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, F or I?

choices.label: D
choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: I has the larger atomic radius: same group; radius increases down a group, so I is larger.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample starts with 100 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 5 days. How much remains after 5 days?
questions.explanation: Number of half-lives = 5/5 = 1; remaining = 100 x (1/2)^1 = 50 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule CCl4 is best described as
questions.explanation: CCl4 is a nonpolar molecule because its symmetric shape makes the bond dipoles cancel, giving no net dipole.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles correspond to 6.02 x 10^22 molecules? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: moles = molecules / N_A = 6.02 x 10^22 / (6.022 x 10^23) = 0.1 mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the higher first ionization energy, Mg or Ba?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ionization energy shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: Mg has the higher first ionization energy: same group; ionization energy decreases down a group, so Mg is higher.

questions.stem: By convention, the direction of electric current is

choices.label: C
choices.text: the direction that positive charge would flow.

choices.label: D
choices.text: always from low to high potential.

questions.explanation: Conventional current is the flow direction of positive charge, opposite to electron drift.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which property of a light wave determines its color?
questions.explanation: Color corresponds to frequency/wavelength; amplitude relates to brightness.

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure CH4?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in CH4 is London dispersion forces.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For the reaction 4 Fe + 3 O2 → 2 Fe2O3, how many moles of Fe2O3 form from 8 mol of Fe (with the other reactant in excess)?
questions.explanation: Use the mole ratio 2 Fe2O3 : 4 Fe: 8 x 2/4 = 4 mol Fe2O3.

questions.stem: Across a period (left to right), atomic radius generally
questions.explanation: Increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons in, so atomic radius decreases across a period.

questions.stem: What net force is required to accelerate a 10 kg object at 0.5 m/s^2?
questions.explanation: Newton's second law: F = m a = (10)(0.5) = 5 N.

questions.stem: A current of 0.1 A flows through a 20 Ω resistor. What is the voltage across it?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I R = (0.1)(20) = 2 V.

questions.stem: Which molecule can form hydrogen bonds with other molecules of itself?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding requires H bonded to N, O, or F; water (O-H) qualifies.

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of Na in NaCl?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (23/58.5) x 100 = 39.3%.

questions.stem: A gas occupies 6 L at 200 K. At constant pressure, what volume does it occupy at 400 K?
questions.explanation: Charles's law: V1/T1 = V2/T2, so V2 = 6 x 400/200 = 12 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = 150 kJ and ΔH2 = 100 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = 150 + 100 = 250 kJ.

questions.explanation: Pure water at 25 degrees C has [H+] = [OH-] = 1 x 10^-7 M, so pH = 7.
